steps.title: 1. 🧠 What is Intelligence?
steps.body:
**Intelligence** is the ability to:

📚 **Learn**

🤔 **Think**

✅ **Make Decisions**




There are **two types of intelligence** we will learn about in this course:

🧠 **Human Intelligence**

🤖 **Artificial Intelligence (AI)**




Let's explore both!

steps.title: 2. 🧠 Human Intelligence
steps.body:
Human Intelligence helps us:

📚 **Learn from Experience**

🤔 **Think About Situations**

✅ **Make Decisions**


Let's see how humans use intelligence every day.






## 📚 Learn from Experience

Imagine you are riding your bicycle.

🚲 One day, you hit a small obstacle and almost fall.

The next day, when you see the same obstacle, you go around it.

👉 **You learned from your experience.**







## 🤔 Think About Situations

Imagine it is raining outside.

🌧️ Before leaving home, you think:

- **Should I take an umbrella?**
- **What if it rains more?**
- **Will I get wet?**

After thinking about the situation, you prepare accordingly.

👉 **You used intelligence to think before acting.**







## ✅ Make Decisions 

Imagine you have:

📖 **Homework to complete**

🎮 **A game you want to play**

You decide to finish your homework first and play later.

👉 **You made a decision.**

steps.title: 3. 🌟 Human Intelligence Summary
steps.body:
Every day, humans use intelligence to:

📚 **Learn from Experience**

🤔 **Think About Situations**

✅ **Make Decisions**

Whether we are studying, playing sports, or solving problems, **we use intelligence all the time.**

steps.title: 4. 🤖 Artificial Intelligence (AI)
steps.body:
**Artificial Intelligence (AI)** is intelligence created by humans.

AI can:

📋 **Follow Instructions**

📚 **Learn from Data**

✅ **Make Decisions or Predictions**


Let's see how AI works.



---



## 1. 📋 Follow Instructions

Imagine you tell a robot:

**"If the room is dark, turn on the light."**

The robot follows your instruction.

👉 **It is doing exactly what you told it to do.**







## 2.  📚 Learn from Data

Imagine an AI sees thousands of pictures of cats.

🐱 🐱 🐱 🐱

Over time, it learns what cats usually look like.

👉 **It learned from data.**







## 3. ✅ Make Decisions or Predictions

Imagine you watch cricket videos every day on YouTube.

🏏 🏏 🏏

Soon, YouTube starts recommending more cricket videos.



👉 **The AI predicts what you might like to watch.**

steps.title: 5. 🌟 Artificial Intelligence Summary
steps.body:
AI can:

📋 **Follow Instructions**

📚 **Learn from Data**

✅ **Make Decisions or Predictions**


AI can perform some tasks that appear intelligent, but it is created by humans.

steps.title: 6. 🚀 Why Do We Build AI?
steps.body:
The goal of AI is to **mimic some parts of human intelligence**.

Why?

⚡ **To Save Time**

📈 **To Improve Efficiency**

🔍 **To Process Large Amounts of Information**

🤝 **To Help Humans Solve Problems**





## 🌟 Remember

🧠 **Humans create AI.**

🤖 **AI helps humans.**

👉 **AI is a tool designed to help us solve problems and make life easier.**

steps.title: 7. 🤔 How Does AI Know What To Do?
steps.body:
We learned that AI can:


- 📋 **Follow Instructions**

- 📚 **Learn from Data**

- ✅ **Make Decisions or Predictions**




| 🧠 Human Intelligence | 🤖 Artificial Intelligence (AI) |
| --- | --- |
| Learns from Experience | Learns from Data |
| Thinks About Situations | Follows Instructions |
| Makes Decisions | Makes Decisions or Predictions |




But who teaches AI what to do?

👉 Humans do!

AI follows instructions called **rules**.



🌟 In the next chapter, we will learn how to teach computers using rules.

steps.title: A Quick History of AI
steps.body:
In 1956, scientists started exploring how computers could become intelligent.

One of their first ideas was simple: teach computers to follow rules.



For example:

- If a **Switch** is ON → Turn the **Bulb** ON.
- If **Motion** is detected → Turn the **Fan** ON.
- If the **Temperature** is above **35°C** → Turn the **AC** ON.


This approach became known as **Rule-Based AI**.



In this chapter, you will build your own Rule-Based AI systems using ABITA.

steps.title: 1. 💡Turn On the Bulb
steps.instruction:
## Goal

Learn how to connect an input to an action.



## Problem
If the **switch** is **ON**, the **bulb** should turn **ON**.


## Instructions

**Step 1:**
Drag **Switch** from the **Rule-Based menu**.

**Step 2:**
Drag **Bulb** from the **Actions** menu.

**Step 3:**
Connect **Switch** to **Bulb**.

**Step 4:**
Turn **ON** the **switch** and observe the result.




## Expected Result

✅ When the **switch** is **ON**, the **bulb** turns **ON**.
✅ When the **switch** is **OFF**, the **bulb** turns **OFF**.




## What You Learned

1. Inputs can control actions.
2. Components can be connected together to create a simple smart system.

steps.title: 2. 🌀Turn On the Fan
steps.instruction:
## Problem

If the **Switch** is **ON**, the **Fan** should turn **ON**.




## Instructions

**Step 1:**
Drag **Switch** from the **Rule-Based menu**.

**Step 2:**
Drag **Fan** from the **Actions** menu.

**Step 3:**
Connect **Switch** to **Fan**.

**Step 4:**
Turn **ON** the **Switch** and observe the result.

**Step 5:**
Turn **OFF** the **Switch** and observe the result.





## Expected Result

✅ When the **Switch** is **ON**, the **Fan** turns **ON**.

✅ When the **Switch** is **OFF**, the **Fan** turns **OFF**.





## What You Learned

1. A **Switch** can control different devices.

2. Inputs can be connected directly to actions to build simple smart systems.

steps.title: 3. 🚪Open the Door
steps.instruction:
## Goal

Learn how to control different devices using the same input.


## Problem

If the **switch** is **ON**, the **door** should **open**.




## Instructions

**Step 1: ** Drag **Switch** from the **Rule-Based menu**.
**Step 2:** Drag **Door** from the **Actions** menu.
**Step 3:** Connect **Switch** to **Door**.
**Step 4:** Turn **ON** the **switch** and observe the result.




## Expected Result

✅ When the **switch** is **ON**, the **door** opens.
✅ When the **switch** is **OFF**, the **door** closes.




## What You Learned

1. The same **input** can control different **actions**.
2. Computers can automate devices such as **lights**, **fans**, and **doors**.

steps.title: Challenge Lab 1: Smart Classroom Starter
steps.prompt:
**Problem**

A teacher wants the classroom to be ready when students arrive.

Turn ON both the **Bulb** and **Fan** using a single **Switch**.

---

**Requirements**

- Use one **Switch**.
- Use a **Bulb**.
- Use a **Fan**.

---

**Success Criteria**

✅ Both the **Bulb** and **Fan** turn ON when the **Switch** is ON.

✅ Both devices turn OFF when the **Switch** is OFF.

steps.title: 4. 🚶Motion Light
steps.instruction:
## Goal

Learn how to use an **IF Condition** with a **Motion Sensor**.




## Problem

If **motion** is detected, turn the **bulb** ON.




## Instructions

- Drag **Motion Sensor** from the **Rule-Based menu**.
- Drag **IF Condition** from the **Rule-Based menu**.
- Drag **Bulb** from the **Actions** menu.
- Connect **Motion Sensor** to the **IF Condition**.
- Connect the **IF Condition** to the **Bulb**.
- Double-click the **IF Condition**.
- Set the condition to **Motion Detected**.
- Double-click the **Motion Sensor**.
- Click the **No Motion** button to change its state.
- Observe the result.




## Expected Result

✅ When **Motion** is detected, the **bulb** turns **ON**.
✅ When **No Motion** is detected, the **bulb** remains **OFF**.




**What You Learned**

1. An **IF Condition** helps computers make decisions.
2. Sensors provide information that can be used in rules.
steps.canvasHint: Click on a block to select it, its settings appear in the Properties panel on the right.

steps.title: 5. 🌬️Smart Fan
steps.instruction:
## Goal

Learn how the same **IF Condition** can control different devices.




## Problem

If **motion** is detected, turn the **fan** ON.




## Instructions

- Drag **Motion Sensor** from the **Rule-Based menu**.
- Drag **IF Condition** from the **Rule-Based menu**.
- Drag **Fan** from the **Actions** menu.
- Connect **Motion Sensor** to the **IF Condition**.
- Connect the **IF Condition** to the **Fan**.
- Double-click the **IF Condition**.
- Set the condition to **Motion Detected**.
- Double-click the **Motion Sensor**.
- Click the **No Motion** button to change its state.
- Observe the result.




## Expected Result

✅ When **Motion** is detected, the **fan** turns **ON**.
✅ When **No Motion** is detected, the **fan** remains **OFF**.




## What You Learned

1. The same **IF Condition** can control different devices.
2. Rules can be reused to automate many actions.

steps.title: Challenge Lab 2: Smart Study Lamp
steps.prompt:
**Problem**

Turn the **Bulb** ON when **Motion** is detected.



**Requirements**

- Use a **Motion Sensor**.
- Use an **IF Condition**.
- Use a **Bulb**.



**Success Criteria**

✅ The **Bulb** turns **ON** when **Motion** is detected.

✅ The **Bulb** remains **OFF** when there is **No Motion**.

steps.title: Challenge Lab 3: Welcome Home System
steps.prompt:
**Problem**

Turn ON both the **Bulb** and **Fan** when **Motion** is detected.



**Requirements**

- Use a **Motion Sensor**.
- Use an **IF Condition**.
- Use a **Bulb**.
- Use a **Fan**.



**Success Criteria**

✅ Both the **Bulb** and **Fan** turn **ON** when **Motion** is detected.

✅ Both devices remain **OFF** when there is **No Motion**.

steps.title: 6. 🥵Hot Room Alert
steps.instruction:
## Problem

If the **temperature** is greater than **35°C**, turn the **fan** ON.




## Instructions

- Drag **Temperature Sensor** from the **Rule-Based menu**.
- Drag **IF Condition** from the **Rule-Based menu**.
- Drag **Fan** from the **Actions** menu.
- Connect **Temperature Sensor** to the **IF Condition**.
- Connect the **IF Condition** to the **Fan**.
- Double-click the **IF Condition**.
- Set the condition to **Temperature > 35°C**.
- Double-click the **Temperature Sensor**.
- Move the slider to **30°C** and observe the result.
- Move the slider to **36°C** and observe the result.




## Expected Result

✅ When the **temperature** is **30°C**, the **fan** remains **OFF**.
✅ When the **temperature** is **36°C**, the **fan** turns **ON**.




## What You Learned

- An **IF Condition** can compare values.
- Computers can make decisions based on sensor readings.

steps.title: 7. ❄️Hot Room Cooling System
steps.instruction:
## Goal

Learn how one **IF Condition** can control multiple devices.



## Problem

If the **temperature** is greater than **40°C**, turn **ON** the **Fan** and the **AC**.



## Instructions


**Step 1:**
Drag **Temperature Sensor** from the **Rule-Based menu**.

**Step 2:**
Drag **IF Condition** from the **Rule-Based menu**.

**Step 3:**
Drag **Fan** from the **Actions** menu.

**Step 4:**
Drag **AC** from the **Actions** menu.

**Step 5:**
Connect **Temperature Sensor** to the **IF Condition**.

**Step 6:**
Connect the **IF Condition** to the **Fan**.

**Step 7:**
Connect the **IF Condition** to the **AC**.

**Step 8:**
Double-click the **IF Condition**.

**Step 9:**
Set the condition to **Temperature > 40°C**.

**Step 10:**
Double-click the **Temperature Sensor**.

**Step 11:**
Move the slider to **35°C** and observe the result.

**Step 12:**
Move the slider to **41°C** and observe the result.





## Expected Result

✅ When the **temperature** is **35°C**, the **Fan** and **AC** remain **OFF**.

✅ When the **temperature** is **41°C**, the **Fan** and **AC** turn **ON**.





## What You Learned

1. One **IF Condition** can control multiple devices.
2. A single rule can trigger multiple actions.

steps.title: 8. 🔑Password Protected Door
steps.instruction:
## Goal

Learn how to use **Text Input** with an **IF Condition**.




## Problem

If the password is **Pakistan**, open the **Door**.




## Instructions


**Step 1:**
Drag **Text Input** from the **Rule-Based menu**.

**Step 2:**
Drag **IF Condition** from the **Rule-Based menu**.

**Step 3:**
Drag **Door** from the **Actions** menu.

**Step 4:**
Connect **Text Input** to the **IF Condition**.

**Step 5:**
Connect the **IF Condition** to the **Door**.

**Step 6:**
Double-click the **IF Condition**.

**Step 7:**
Set the condition to **Text = Pakistan**.

**Step 8:**
Double-click the **Text Input**.

**Step 9:**
Enter **pakistan** and observe the result.

**Step 10:**
Enter **Pakistan** and observe the result.






## Expected Result

✅ When the text is **pakistan**, the **Door** remains **closed**.

✅ When the text is **Pakistan**, the **Door** opens.





## What You Learned

1. Text can be used as an input for rules.

2. An **IF Condition** can compare words as well as numbers.

3. Text matching is **case-sensitive**, so **Pakistan** and **pakistan** are treated as different values.

steps.title: 9. 👋Smart Greeting Detector
steps.instruction:
## Goal

Learn how to use the **Contains** condition with **Text Input**.




## Problem

If the text contains the word **hello**, turn the **Bulb** ON.




## Instructions

**Step 1:**
Drag **Text Input** from the **Rule-Based menu**.

**Step 2:**
Drag **IF Condition** from the **Rule-Based menu**.

**Step 3:**
Drag **Bulb** from the **Actions** menu.

**Step 4:**
Connect **Text Input** to the **IF Condition**.

**Step 5:**
Connect the **IF Condition** to the **Bulb**.

**Step 6:**
Double-click the **IF Condition** to edit it.

**Step 7:**
From the **Condition** dropdown, select **Contains**.

**Step 8:**
In the **Value to compare** field, type **hello**.

**Step 9:**
Double-click the **Text Input** component.

**Step 10:**
Enter **Good morning everyone** and observe the result.

**Step 11:**
Enter **hello everyone** and observe the result.




## Expected Result

✅ When the text is **Good morning everyone**, the **Bulb** remains **OFF**.

✅ When the text is **hello everyone**, the **Bulb** turns **ON**.




## What You Learned

1. The **Contains** condition can search for a word inside a sentence.

2. A sentence can trigger an action even when it contains additional words.

3. Text matching is **case-sensitive**, so **hello** and **Hello** are treated as different values.

steps.title: Challenge Lab 4: Secure Door
steps.prompt:
**Problem**

Open the **Door** only when the password is **Pakistan**.



**Requirements**

- Use **Text Input**.
- Use an **IF Condition**.
- Use a **Door**.



**Success Criteria**

✅ The **Door** opens when the text is **Pakistan**.

✅ The **Door** remains closed for any other text.

steps.title: 10. 🎚️Two Switch AC System
steps.instruction:
## Goal

Learn how to use an **AND Condition**.





## Problem

Turn the **AC** ON only when **both switches** are ON.




## Instructions


**Step 1:**
Drag **Switch** from the **Rule-Based menu**.

**Step 2:**
Drag another **Switch** from the **Rule-Based menu**.

**Step 3:**
Drag **AND Condition** from the **Rule-Based menu**.

**Step 4:**
Drag **AC** from the **Actions** menu.

**Step 5:**
Connect the first **Switch** to the **AND Condition**.

**Step 6:**
Connect the second **Switch** to the **AND Condition**.

**Step 7:**
Connect the **AND Condition** to the **AC**.

**Step 8:**
Turn ON only the first **Switch** and observe the result.

**Step 9:**
Turn ON only the second **Switch** and observe the result.

**Step 10:**
Turn ON both **Switches** and observe the result.





## Expected Result

✅ When only one **Switch** is ON, the **AC** remains **OFF**.

✅ When both **Switches** are ON, the **AC** turns **ON**.





## What You Learned

1. An **AND Condition** requires all conditions to be true.

2. The output turns ON only when both inputs are ON.

steps.title: 11. 💡Backup Light System
steps.instruction:
## Goal
  
Learn how to use an **OR Condition**.



## Problem
  
A room has **two switches**.

The **Light** should turn ON if **either Switch A OR Switch B** is ON.

This way, if one switch fails, the other switch can still turn the Light ON.





## Instructions

**Step 1:**  
Drag **Switch** from the **Rule-Based menu**.

**Step 2:**  
Drag another **Switch** from the **Rule-Based menu**.

**Step 3:**  
Drag **OR Condition** from the **Rule-Based menu**.

**Step 4:**  
Drag **Bulb** from the **Actions menu**.

**Step 5:**  
Connect the first **Switch** to the **OR Condition**.

**Step 6:**  
Connect the second **Switch** to the **OR Condition**.

**Step 7:**  
Connect the **OR Condition** to the **Bulb**.

**Step 8:**  
Turn ON only the first **Switch** and observe the result.

**Step 9:**  
Turn ON only the second **Switch** and observe the result.

**Step 10:**  
Turn OFF both **Switches** and observe the result.





## Expected Result

✅ When the first **Switch** is ON, the **Bulb** turns **ON**.

✅ When the second **Switch** is ON, the **Bulb** turns **ON**.

✅ When both **Switches** are ON, the **Bulb** turns **ON**.

❌ When both **Switches** are OFF, the **Bulb** remains **OFF**.





## What You Learned

1. An **OR Condition** requires at least one condition to be true.

2. The output turns **ON** when either input is **ON**.

3. **OR Conditions** are useful for creating backup systems.

steps.title: 12. 🛡️Security Door
steps.instruction:
## Goal

Learn how to use a **NOT Condition**.





## Problem

Open the **Door** only when the **Switch** is OFF.



## Instructions

**Step 1:**
Drag **Switch** from the **Rule-Based menu**.

**Step 2:**
Drag **NOT Condition** from the **Rule-Based menu**.

**Step 3:**
Drag **Door** from the **Actions** menu.

**Step 4:**
Connect the **Switch** to the **NOT Condition**.

**Step 5:**
Connect the **NOT Condition** to the **Door**.

**Step 6:**
Turn the **Switch** OFF and observe the result.

**Step 7:**
Turn the **Switch** ON and observe the result.




## Expected Result

✅ When the **Switch** is **OFF**, the **Door** opens.

✅ When the **Switch** is **ON**, the **Door** remains closed.





## What You Learned

1. A **NOT Condition** reverses a result.

2. If something is **TRUE**, **NOT** makes it **FALSE**.

3. If something is **FALSE**, **NOT** makes it **TRUE**.

steps.title: Challenge Lab 5: Security Approval System
steps.prompt:
**Problem**

Turn the **AC** ON only when:

- **Switch A** is ON.
- **Switch B** is ON.
- **Motion** is detected.



**Requirements**

- Use **2 Switches**.
- Use a **Motion Sensor**.
- Use **IF Conditions**.
- Use **2** **AND Conditions**.
- Use an **AC**.



**Success Criteria**

✅ The **AC** turns **ON** only when all three conditions are TRUE.

✅ The **AC** remains **OFF** if any condition is FALSE.

steps.title: 13. 🌡️Comfort Zone Temperature Control
steps.instruction:
## Goal
Learn how to combine two **IF Conditions** using an **AND Condition**.




## Problem

Turn the **Fan** ON only when the **temperature** is between **30°C** and **35°C**.





## Instructions

**Step 1:**
Drag **Temperature Sensor** from the **Rule-Based menu**.

**Step 2:**
Drag **IF Condition** from the **Rule-Based menu**.

**Step 3:**
Drag another **IF Condition** from the **Rule-Based menu**.

**Step 4:**
Drag **AND Condition** from the **Rule-Based menu**.

**Step 5:**
Drag **Fan** from the **Actions** menu.

**Step 6:**
Connect the **Temperature Sensor** to both **IF Conditions**.

**Step 7:**
Connect both **IF Conditions** to the **AND Condition**.

**Step 8:**
Connect the **AND Condition** to the **Fan**.

**Step 9:**
Double-click the first **IF Condition**.

**Step 10:**
Set the condition to **Temperature > 30°C**.

**Step 11:**
Double-click the second **IF Condition**.

**Step 12:**
Set the condition to **Temperature < 35°C**.

**Step 13:**
Double-click the **Temperature Sensor**.

**Step 14:**
Move the slider to **25°C** and observe the result.

**Step 15:**
Move the slider to **32°C** and observe the result.

**Step 16:**
Move the slider to **40°C** and observe the result.






## Expected Result

✅ When the **temperature** is **25°C**, the **Fan** remains **OFF**.

✅ When the **temperature** is **32°C**, the **Fan** turns **ON**.

✅ When the **temperature** is **40°C**, the **Fan** remains **OFF**.






## What You Learned

- Two **IF Conditions** can be combined using an **AND Condition**.

- The **AND Condition** becomes TRUE only when both conditions are TRUE.

- Computers can use ranges to make smarter decisions.

steps.title: 14. 🏠Occupied Room Cooling System
steps.instruction:
## Goal

Learn how to combine multiple **IF Conditions** using an **AND Condition**.



## Problem

Turn the **AC** ON only when **motion is detected** and the **temperature** is greater than **35°C**.



## Instructions


**Step 1:**
Drag **Motion Sensor** from the **Rule-Based menu**.

**Step 2:**
Drag **Temperature Sensor** from the **Rule-Based menu**.

**Step 3:**
Drag **IF Condition** from the **Rule-Based menu**.

**Step 4:**
Drag another **IF Condition** from the **Rule-Based menu**.

**Step 5:**
Drag **AND Condition** from the **Rule-Based menu**.

**Step 6:**
Drag **AC** from the **Actions** menu.

**Step 7:**
Connect the **Motion Sensor** to the first **IF Condition**.

**Step 8:**
Connect the **Temperature Sensor** to the second **IF Condition**.

**Step 9:**
Connect both **IF Conditions** to the **AND Condition**.

**Step 10:**
Connect the **AND Condition** to the **AC**.

**Step 11:**
Double-click the first **IF Condition**.

**Step 12:**
Set the condition to **Motion Detected**.

**Step 13:**
Double-click the second **IF Condition**.

**Step 14:**
Set the condition to **Temperature > 35°C**.

**Step 15:**
Double-click the **Motion Sensor** and set it to **No Motion**.

**Step 16:**
Double-click the **Temperature Sensor** and move the slider to **40°C**.

**Step 17:**
Observe the result.

**Step 18:**
Change the **Motion Sensor** to **Motion Detected**.

**Step 19:**
Observe the result.






## Expected Result


✅ When the **temperature** is **40°C** but there is **No Motion**, the **AC** remains **OFF**.

✅ When **Motion** is detected and the **temperature** is **40°C**, the **AC** turns **ON**.




## What You Learned

Multiple **IF Conditions** can be combined using an **AND Condition**.

The **AND Condition** becomes TRUE only when all connected conditions are TRUE.

Smart systems often use multiple sensors before taking action.

steps.title: Challenge Lab 6: Comfort Zone Fan
steps.prompt:
**Problem**

Turn the **Fan** ON only when the temperature is between **30°C** and **35°C**.



**Requirements**

- Use a **Temperature Sensor**.
- Use **2 IF Conditions**.
- Use an **AND Condition**.
- Use a **Fan**.



**Success Criteria**

✅ The **Fan** turns **ON** when the temperature is between **30°C** and **35°C**.

✅ The **Fan** remains **OFF** outside this range.

steps.title: 15. 🏡Smart Room Assistant
steps.instruction:
## Goal

Learn how to build a smart system using multiple rules.





## Problem

- If **motion** is detected, turn the **Bulb** ON.
- If the **temperature** is greater than **35°C**, turn the **AC** ON.





## Instructions


**Step 1:**
Drag **Motion Sensor** from the **Rule-Based menu**.

**Step 2:**
Drag **Temperature Sensor** from the **Rule-Based menu**.

**Step 3:**
Drag **IF Condition** from the **Rule-Based menu**.

**Step 4:**
Drag another **IF Condition** from the **Rule-Based menu**.

**Step 5:**
Drag **Bulb** from the **Actions** menu.

**Step 6:**
Drag **AC** from the **Actions** menu.

**Step 7:**
Connect the **Motion Sensor** to the first **IF Condition**.

**Step 8:**
Connect the first **IF Condition** to the **Bulb**.

**Step 9:**
Connect the **Temperature Sensor** to the second **IF Condition**.

**Step 10:**
Connect the second **IF Condition** to the **AC**.

**Step 11:**
Double-click the first **IF Condition**.

**Step 12:**
Set the condition to **Motion Detected**.

**Step 13:**
Double-click the second **IF Condition**.

**Step 14:**
Set the condition to **Temperature > 35°C**.

**Step 15:**
Double-click the **Motion Sensor** and set it to **Motion Detected**.

**Step 16:**
Double-click the **Temperature Sensor** and move the slider to **40°C**.

**Step 17:**
Observe the result.





## Expected Result

✅ When **Motion** is detected, the **Bulb** turns **ON**.

✅ When the **temperature** is above **35°C**, the **AC** turns **ON**.

✅ Both rules work independently at the same time.





## What You Learned

1. A smart system can contain multiple rules.

2. Different sensors can control different devices.

3. Complex systems are built by combining simple rules.

steps.title: Challenge Lab 7: Smart Classroom
steps.prompt:
**Problem**

Create a **Smart Classroom** that automatically controls lights and cooling.



**Requirements**

- If **Motion** is detected → Turn **Bulb** ON.
- If **Temperature > 35°C** → Turn **Fan** ON.
- If **Temperature > 40°C** → Turn **AC** ON.



**Allowed Components**

- **1 Motion Sensor**
- **1 Temperature Sensor**
- **1 Bulb**
- **1 Fan**
- **1 AC**
- Any number of **IF Conditions**
- Any number of **AND**, **OR**, and **NOT Conditions**



**Success Criteria**

✅ The **Bulb** responds to **Motion**.

✅ The **Fan** responds to **Temperature**.

✅ The **AC** responds to higher **Temperature**.

✅ Only the allowed components are used.

✅ All rules work together correctly.

steps.title: 16. 📌Summary
steps.body:
Great job!

In this chapter, you learned that:



📋 **AI follows instructions.**

🔀 **Rules use IF–THEN logic.**

🤖 **AI follows rules exactly as they are written.**

✅ **The same input produces the same output.**

✅ **Rules work well in simple and predictable situations.**




Using ABITA, you built your own **rule-based AI systems** and taught AI how to respond to different situations.



🌟 **You are now ready to explore a bigger question: Can rules solve every problem?**

steps.title: 1. 🤔 When Rules Are Not Enough
steps.body:
In the last chapter, you taught AI using **rules**.

You learned that rules can help AI make decisions.

But can rules solve **every problem**?


Let's find out!

steps.title: 2. ⚠️ What is a Spam Detector?
steps.body:
# 📧 What is a Spam Detector?

Imagine your inbox receives 100 messages today.

Some messages are important:

📧 "Your teacher shared today's homework."

📧 "Your football match starts at 5 PM."





But some messages are unwanted:

📧 "Congratulations! You won a prize!"

📧 "Click here to claim your reward!"

📧 "You have won $1,000,000!"




A Spam Detector helps separate unwanted messages from important ones so we do not miss something important.

steps.title: 3. 🚀 Your Mission
steps.body:
🚀 **Your Mission**

Can you build a spam detector using only **rules**?



👉 In this chapter, we will discover whether simple rules are enough to build a good spam detector.

steps.title: 4. Guided Lab 1: Detect a Spam Word
steps.instruction:
## Goal

Learn how a rule can be used to detect a spam message.




## Problem

You work for an email company.

Your security team has discovered that many spam emails contain the word:



**MONEY**

Create a spam detector that triggers the Alarm whenever this word appears.




## Instructions


**Step 1:**

Drag a **Text Input** from the **Rule-Based** menu.

**Step 2:**

Drag an **IF Condition** from the **Rule-Based** menu.

**Step 3:**

Drag an **Alarm** from the **Actions** menu.

**Step 4:**

Connect:

**Text Input → IF Condition**

**IF Condition → Alarm**

**Step 5:**

Double-click the **IF Condition**.

Configure it as:

**Contains: MONEY**

**Step 6:**

Go to the **Text Input** and type:

**MONEY**

Observe the result.

**Step 7:**

Now type:

**mOneY**

Observe the result.

The Alarm should remain silent.



## Success Criteria

✅ Alarm turns ON when the message contains **MONEY**.

✅ Alarm remains OFF for messages that do not contain **MONEY**.

steps.title: 5. Guided Lab 2: Spammers Found a New Variation
steps.instruction:
## Goal

Learn how rule-based systems need additional rules when new variations appear.




## Problem

Your spam detector successfully detects: **MONEY**

However, spammers have discovered a way to bypass your system.

Instead of writing: **MONEY**

they now write: **MonEy**

Update your spam detector so it can detect both versions.




## Instructions

**Step 1:**

Open your existing spam detector.

**Step 2:**

Drag a new **IF Condition** from the **Rule-Based** menu.

**Step 3:**

Drag an **OR Condition** from the **Rule-Based** menu.

**Step 4:**

Connect both **IF Conditions** to the **OR Condition**.

**Step 5:**

Connect the **OR Condition** to the **Alarm**.

**Step 6:**

Double-click the new **IF Condition**.

Configure it as:

**Contains: MonEy**

**Step 7:**

Go to the **Text Input** and type:

**MONEY**

Observe the result.

The Alarm should turn ON.

**Step 8:**

Now type:

**MonEy**

Observe the result.

The Alarm should turn ON.



## Success Criteria

✅ Alarm turns ON when the message contains **MONEY**.

✅ Alarm turns ON when the message contains **MonEy**.

✅ Alarm remains OFF for other messages.




## Expected Result

🤔 Your detector can now catch two variations of the same spam word, but it requires additional rules to do so.

steps.title: Challenge Lab 1: Improve the Spam Detector
steps.prompt:
## Problem

Spammers have created another variation:

**money**

Update your spam detector so it can detect:

- **MONEY**
- **MonEy**
- **money**



## Requirements

- Use the existing spam detector.
- Use an **IF Condition**.
- Use **OR Conditions** if required.
- Use an **Alarm**.



## Success Criteria

✅ Alarm turns ON when the message contains **MONEY**.

✅ Alarm turns ON when the message contains **MonEy**.

✅ Alarm turns ON when the message contains **money**.

✅ Alarm remains OFF for messages that do not contain these words.

steps.title: Challenge Lab 2: Keep Up with the Spammers
steps.prompt:
## Problem

Spammers have created another variation:

**MONey**

Update your spam detector so it can detect this variation as well.



## Requirements

- Use the existing spam detector.
- Use an **IF Condition**.
- Use **OR Conditions**.
- Use an **Alarm**.



## Success Criteria

✅ Alarm turns ON when the message contains **MONEY**.

✅ Alarm turns ON when the message contains **MonEy**.

✅ Alarm turns ON when the message contains **money**.

✅ Alarm turns ON when the message contains **MoNeY**.

✅ Alarm turns ON when the message contains **MONey**.

✅ Alarm remains OFF for messages that do not contain these words.

steps.title: 6. 🧩 The Rule Problem
steps.body:
So far, we have been trying to detect the word:

**MONEY**


At first, this seemed easy.

We created one rule:

**IF Text = MONEY → Alarm**



But then spammers started using different variations:

- **Money**
- **money**
- **MonEy**
- **MoNeY**
- **MONey**
- **mONeY**
- **moneY**


For every new variation, we had to add another rule.

steps.title: 7. 🔄 More Variations, More Rules
steps.body:
## How Many Rules Do We Need?

The word **MONEY** has 5 letters.

Each letter can be **uppercase** or **lowercase**.

This means there are many possible variations.

Just to detect one word, we may need dozens of **IF Conditions**.

And we still might miss some variations.
steps.tip:
The word "MONEY" has 5 letters.

Each letter can be uppercase or lowercase.

This means there are:

2 × 2 × 2 × 2 × 2 = 32 possible variations.

Would you create 32 different IF Conditions just to detect one word?

steps.title: 8. 🚀 The Bigger Word Problem
steps.body:
## What About a Longer Word?

Now imagine we want to detect:

**CONGRATULATIONS**

Possible variations include:

- **Congratulations**
- **CONGRATULATIONS**
- **congratulations**
- **CoNgRaTuLaTiOnS**

And many more.

Would we create a new rule for every variation?

That would require a lot of IF Conditions.
steps.tip:
The word "CONGRATULATIONS" has 15 letters.

That means it can be written in:

"32,768" different ways.

Would you create 32,768 "IF Conditions" for a single word?

steps.title: 9. 💬 The Sentence Problem
steps.body:
## What About Full Sentences?

Now imagine we want to detect this spam message:

**Congratulations! You have won a free prize.**

A spammer could write:

- **Congrats! You won a reward.**
- **Claim your free gift now!**
- **You have been selected for a special offer!**

All of these messages have a similar meaning, but they use different words.

Would we need hundreds or even thousands of rules to detect every possible sentence?

steps.title: 10. 🐱 A Bigger Challenge
steps.body:
## Let's Try Something Different


Imagine we want a computer to detect a picture of a **CAT**.


We could create rules like:

- IF the animal has 2 ears
- IF the animal has 4 legs
- IF the animal has a tail
- IF the animal has whiskers


Then it must be a CAT.


But there is a problem.

A **DOG** can also have:

- 2 ears
- 4 legs
- A tail

Now we need even more rules.

How many rules would we need to correctly identify every CAT and every DOG?


🌟 In the next chapter, we will explore a different way to teach AI.

steps.title: 📌 Summary
steps.body:
## What We Learned

- Rules can help computers make decisions.
- A spam detector can be created using IF Conditions.
- Every new variation requires additional rules.
- Longer words need even more rules.
- Full sentences can be written in many different ways.
- As rules increase, systems become larger and harder to manage.


📌 Sometimes writing more rules is not the best solution.



🌟 What if we could teach AI without writing thousands of rules?

How would that work?

Let's find out in the next chapter.
